headline: Does Microsoft’s Massive AI Deal And GPU Build‑Out Change The Bull Case For IREN (IREN)?
summary: Iren recently accelerated its shift from Bitcoin mining to AI cloud infrastructure, securing a five‑year US$9.70 billion Microsoft AI data center capacity deal and arranging US$9.30 billion of funding to deploy more than 150,000 Nvidia GPUs across a 4.50 GW power portfolio. This combination of a large, long-term hyperscale contract and substantial capital raising highlights both Iren’s potential to scale AI revenues and the execution and dilution risks that come with such rapid...

headline: NVIDIA’s In Orbit AI Milestone Extends Reach Across Key Infrastructure Markets
summary: Planet Labs completed real-time AI object detection onboard its Pelican-4 satellite using NVIDIA Jetson Orin modules, marking a first for in-orbit AI inference. The AI workload ran directly in space instead of after data download to Earth, aiming to shorten the delay between image capture and actionable insight. NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) is also supplying hardware and platforms for partners in telecommunications and industry clouds that are building AI capable networks and data...

headline: Broadcom Joins AI Cybersecurity Drive As Valuation And Growth Draw Focus
summary: Broadcom has joined Project Glasswing, an industry wide cybersecurity initiative focused on using advanced AI models to protect critical software infrastructure. The collaboration brings together Broadcom, Amazon, Anthropic, Apple, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia and others to apply AI to vulnerability detection and defensive security tools. The move extends Broadcom's role beyond its core semiconductor and AI chip business into infrastructure software and cybersecurity partnerships. For...

headline: Super Micro Board Probes Export Allegations As Valuation Signals Potential Upside
summary: Super Micro Computer (NasdaqGS:SMCI) has launched an independent investigation into alleged illegal exports of AI hardware to China linked to three individuals, including a co founder and senior staff. The company is not named as a defendant in the March 2026 indictment. Those involved have been placed on leave and key relationships have been terminated while the review is underway. Independent board members and external legal counsel have been appointed to oversee the investigation. For...
